Open-source fonts bundled with the app —: Open-source fonts bundled with the app — nothing to download, nothing fetched.
Understand the world as it really is.
Local · Offline · Nothing leaves this ma: Local · Offline · Nothing leaves this machine
For journalists working under pressure. These tools are honest about their limits — read each note. Everything stays on this machine.
An encrypted snapshot of the whole corpu: An encrypted snapshot of the whole corpus (AES-256-GCM + scrypt). A lost or wrong passphrase means the file cannot be opened — there is no recovery. Keep the passphrase safe.
Transparent mode names this tool in the: Transparent mode names this tool in the User-Agent (polite, but identifiable). Protected mode sends a generic User-Agent and routes every fetch through a proxy you run.
Open this external link in your browser?: Open this external link in your browser?
This leaves the app and contacts an outs: This leaves the app and contacts an outside server.
A shareable synthesis of how the app see: A shareable synthesis of how the app sees your corpus — for improving the next version. Generated only when you click, and only you decide who gets the file.
All gathered keywords with real counts,: All gathered keywords with real counts, the computed keyword families, your merge/split corrections and super-groups — exactly what keyword-grouping fixes need to see. Counts and structures only, never scores.
This word or name suddenly appears much: This word or name suddenly appears much more often in the articles you collected than it did before. A jump like that usually means a story is moving.
Some of the price feeds you rely on have: Some of the price feeds you rely on have stopped delivering fresh numbers, or reported an error on their last run. The figures they show may be out of date until they run again.
Several of your sources published almost: Several of your sources published almost exactly the same text on the same story. That can be a shared newswire — or coordination. Either way, those voices may count as one, not many.
Mentions in the 30 days before that: Mentions in the 30 days before that
Brand-new term — no earlier mentions to: Brand-new term — no earlier mentions to compare against
How sure can we be? (95% interval): How sure can we be? (95% interval)
Too few mentions for a confidence interv: Too few mentions for a confidence interval
Terms scanned to pick the top few: Terms scanned to pick the top few
Articles collected in the last 30 days: Articles collected in the last 30 days
Of which, from your top 3 sources: Of which, from your top 3 sources
Top-3 share = top-3 articles ÷ all artic: Top-3 share = top-3 articles ÷ all articles
Of which, from the single biggest origin: Of which, from the single biggest origin
Its share = its articles ÷ all articles: Its share = its articles ÷ all articles
Fastest source: articles per day (last 1: Fastest source: articles per day (last 14 days)
Typical source in your corpus (median pe: Typical source in your corpus (median per day)
Must also be many times the typical rate: Must also be many times the typical rate
A feed counts as cold after this many da: A feed counts as cold after this many days
Hours between the first and the median c: Hours between the first and the median copy
Everything that shapes how the app looks: Everything that shapes how the app looks and behaves on this machine. Pick a section — your choices stay local.
UI language. Stored locally; untranslate: UI language. Stored locally; untranslated strings fall back to English.
Your look, your machine. These settings: Your look, your machine. These settings stay in this browser — nothing is sent anywhere.
Machine-drafted translation — the Englis: Machine-drafted translation — the English original is authoritative. Found a better wording? Improve it on the project page.
Measured from this app’s own responses (: Measured from this app’s own responses (bytes ÷ transfer time) — not a system network counter.
Major stock-exchange indices from offici: Major stock-exchange indices from official, free sources (FRED, incl. the FRED·OECD share-price indices) — end-of-day values, each labelled with its as-of date and source.
Every number comes straight from the dow: Every number comes straight from the downloaded file — nothing scraped or guessed.
A bundled directory of ~500 public calen: A bundled directory of ~500 public calendar feeds (country holidays, religions, elections, UN days, space, astronomy). Nothing is fetched until you click.
When several providers publish the same: When several providers publish the same calendar, they are grouped into one folder — you see the duplicate and can compare every source.
not enough points in this window: not enough points in this window
e.g. Climate change — or paste https://e: e.g. Climate change — or paste https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/…
The wiki reports no page with this exact: The wiki reports no page with this exact title — check the spelling, or paste the article URL.
The network log holds every robots and r: The network log holds every robots and reachability verdict for the default sources, market feeds and calendar feeds — share it to help optimize the default install's lists.
The debug bundle is the full picture for: The debug bundle is the full picture for bug reports: runtime facts, scheduler history, every network verdict, what each Load/Refresh click actually did, law/wiki tracking states and the rolling error log — click through the app, then download and share it.
Added to draft — click to open it.
Font size = shared-article volume · dist: Font size = shared-article volume · distance = association strength.
Drag to pan · scroll to zoom (far out go: Drag to pan · scroll to zoom (far out goes up a level) · click a word to dive in.
Set both to re-map the whole slider onto: Set both to re-map the whole slider onto that period (e.g. the last two months) — play then sweeps only it. Clear to return to all recorded time.
Branches grow outward from the centre; e: Branches grow outward from the centre; each leaf hangs off its strongest relative.
Back online — network requests allowed a: Back online — network requests allowed again.
Offline — every new network request is r: Offline — every new network request is refused. One in-flight request may finish.
filter countries by name, code or topic…: filter countries by name, code or topic…

every listed country has at least one so: every listed country has at least one source

Summit/meeting dates move each year — fo: Summit/meeting dates move each year — follow the official source for the exact date.
Manage calendars & sources in Settings →: Manage calendars & sources in Settings → Agenda.
This database is encrypted at rest (SQLC: This database is encrypted at rest (SQLCipher 4). Enter THE passphrase to open it.
Your corpus will be encrypted at rest by: Your corpus will be encrypted at rest by default (SQLCipher 4). Pick ONE stable passphrase — you will enter it at every start, like a user ID.
Choose something unique and remember it.
There is no recovery and no decryption a: There is no recovery and no decryption alternative. A lost passphrase costs re-collection time — the corpus is rebuilt from the web, not from us.
The corpus is open. You can return to th: The corpus is open. You can return to the Console.
At-rest encryption protects a seized or: At-rest encryption protects a seized or copied file.
It cannot protect a compromised running: It cannot protect a compromised running session.
There is no recovery and no decryption a: There is no recovery and no decryption alternative.
A plaintext snapshot is kept as your esc: A plaintext snapshot is kept as your escape hatch — delete it once you have unlocked successfully.
I understand there is no recovery: I understand there is no recovery
Encrypting — this rewrites the whole dat: Encrypting — this rewrites the whole database…
Encrypted. Your passphrase is now requir: Encrypted. Your passphrase is now required at every start — there is no recovery.
Make it long — several unrelated words b: Make it long — several unrelated words beat one clever word. If your database file is copied it can be guessed offline, so length is the real protection (not how fast this screen answers).
Compacting rebuilds the database file: i: Compacting rebuilds the database file: it reclaims the space deletions leave behind and defragments the indexes. It takes time proportional to the file size and pauses collection writes while it runs — nothing is interpreted or removed.
Compacting… this can take a while on a l: Compacting… this can take a while on a large corpus.
Add a watched Wikipedia page (fetches it: Add a watched Wikipedia page (fetches its current revision)
Airplane mode — filled means offline: ev: Airplane mode — filled means offline: every new network request is refused instantly; one in-flight request may finish.
Beyond these local addresses, the intern: Beyond these local addresses, the internet sees whatever public address your ISP or VPN presents — this app does not check it, because checking would itself be a network call.
Fetch market and index data from the off: Fetch market and index data from the official feeds
No non-loopback network interfaces were: No non-loopback network interfaces were found.

Your machine presents these local networ: Your machine presents these local network addresses:
The performance report carries real evid: The performance report carries real evidence from this machine: per-endpoint response times from your actual use since the app started, store and memory facts, and a quick timed self-test over the heavy views of your real corpus. Local only; runs in a few seconds and nothing is transmitted.
One file carries everything: articles, k: One file carries everything: articles, keywords, Wikipedia and law snapshots, market series, events, settings, annotations and the custody log — with a signed manifest, so tampering is detectable. Encrypted with a passphrase by default; there is no recovery for a lost passphrase.
An unencrypted archive protects nothing: An unencrypted archive protects nothing at rest and deliberately excludes your signing keys.
Restoring merges the archive into your c: Restoring merges the archive into your corpus — nothing is replaced or deleted, duplicates are detected bit-for-bit, and where a value conflicts your local version is kept and both values are reported. Preview first: the plan below shows exactly what would happen. A safety snapshot is taken before anything is applied, and the merge refuses to proceed if verification fails.

verification FAILED — merge will be refu: verification FAILED — merge will be refused

No background jobs — every network task: No background jobs — every network task appears here when it runs.
Stopping collection engages the network: Stopping collection engages the network kill switch — the app goes offline.
Start anyway? (Cancel waits — the runnin: Start anyway? (Cancel waits — the running task keeps the bandwidth and the database writer to itself.)

AND, OR, NOT, “quoted phrases” and (pare: AND, OR, NOT, “quoted phrases” and (parentheses) refine the article search. Everything is searched on this machine only — typing here never touches the network.

Reading happens entirely on this machine: Reading happens entirely on this machine: the dump's index is scanned for the title, then one small block is decompressed at its byte offset. The text shown is raw wikitext (unrendered) as of the dump date — a snapshot, not the live article.
No readable dump yet — download a multis: No readable dump yet — download a multistream dump above; its index rides along automatically.
This title is not in the dump's index.
No readable dump for this edition: the m: No readable dump for this edition: the multistream file or its index is missing.
An older single-stream file exists but c: An older single-stream file exists but cannot be random-accessed — re-download to enable reading.
Raw wikitext (unrendered), extracted loc: Raw wikitext (unrendered), extracted locally from your downloaded dump — no network call.

Articles in your corpus citing this URL:: Articles in your corpus citing this URL:

No local record of this link yet.
Built from your local database only — no: Built from your local database only — no network call.
The transparent outbound link — its text: The transparent outbound link — its text is the full address; opening it leaves this machine:
Opens the local preview first — what you: Opens the local preview first — what your database knows about this link
Watched pages join your corpus as articl: Watched pages join your corpus as articles: the newest version is what search and analytics see (the exact revision id is recorded), the full text of every tracked revision is stored on this machine — so past versions stay reconstructable, at the cost of storage growing with edit activity — and the change history remains available per page.
Why these choices: showing the newest ve: Why these choices: showing the newest version keeps analysis aligned with what readers see today; storing every revision's full text makes any past version exactly reconstructable on this machine (version-anchored analytics — no diff replay, no guessing); the page text is wikitext reduced to plain text for keywords, never passed off as the rendered article; everything stays local.
You're offline. Switch off airplane mode: You're offline. Switch off airplane mode to go online and start collecting.
Get started by registering the curated s: Get started by registering the curated source catalog and running a first ingestion. Everything is ethical (robots.txt fail-closed, rate-limited) and local.

Analytics over the articles your search: Analytics over the articles your search matched — counts only, never a verdict.

Nothing running right now — active tasks: Nothing running right now — active tasks (a collection pass, downloads, the fetch on the wire) appear here.

The whole interface switches as you pick: The whole interface switches as you pick. You can change this anytime in Settings.
Pick the language for the whole interfac: Pick the language for the whole interface. The native name is the real identifier — flags are only a visual cue.

The app starts offline. Nothing connects: The app starts offline. Nothing connects until you choose to go online — and you confirm before anything connects.
The one-time guided welcome that introdu: The one-time guided welcome that introduces the app and helps you go online for your first collection (your language and passphrase are chosen on the unlock screen). You can re-open it anytime, or have it show again the next time the app loads.
Show the one-time guided setup again the: Show the one-time guided setup again the next time the app loads.
No collection scheduled or running right: No collection scheduled or running right now — when collection is on, the schedule appears here.
Continuous: passes run back-to-back with: Continuous: passes run back-to-back with only a short gap while online. Going offline stops the loop.
Legacy cadence: one pass, then wait the: Legacy cadence: one pass, then wait the interval before the next.
Computed as the last run plus the inter-: Computed as the last run plus the inter-pass gap; robots delays can stretch it.
These are the scheduler’s own facts — th: These are the scheduler’s own facts — the schedule is managed in Settings. Times are relative; hover for the exact local moment and the method.
Your corpus stays on this machine — no c: Your corpus stays on this machine — no cloud, no telemetry; fetching follows your Network mode.
Generated by a local model — verify agai: Generated by a local model — verify against the stored article.
Equal view — every source is counted onc: Equal view — every source is counted once; no source is de-amplified in this version.
The app gathers and measures; you judge.
Your library is empty — head to Collect: Your library is empty — head to Collect to gather your first material.
Name: Name
The same event carried by several feeds: The same event carried by several feeds is shown once, listing every source; a date disagreement stays visible as separate entries.
Upload a local .ics file — no network. I: Upload a local .ics file — no network. Its events join your agenda (deduplicated) as a removable calendar; the file is parsed and discarded.
All articles match their capture-time ha: All articles match their capture-time hash.
articles diverge from their capture-time: articles diverge from their capture-time hash — evidence of tampering or bit-rot. Nothing was changed.
Re-hash every stored article and compare: Re-hash every stored article and compare to the hash recorded when it was captured. A mismatch means the stored content no longer matches its capture-time hash — evidence of tampering or bit-rot. Nothing is changed; the check is local and read-only.
Fetch a calendar from a URL you provided: Fetch a calendar from a URL you provided
Signed, reproducible record of the query: Signed, reproducible record of the query + method behind this analysis
Signed, tamper-evident bundle of the ana: Signed, tamper-evident bundle of the analysed articles
How many countries the source catalog re: How many countries the source catalog reaches (by each source's country, scored against ISO 3166). Measured from the data; refreshes live.
No tracked changes yet — “Track changes: No tracked changes yet — “Track changes now”.
Region is the source's country/city (the: Region is the source's country/city (the reliable source-based signal).
A consistent snapshot of the entire corp: A consistent snapshot of the entire corpus, taken live via SQLite's online-backup API.
Fetching is ethical: robots.txt is respe: Fetching is ethical: robots.txt is respected (fail-closed) and rate-limited.
Found offline by the citation and catalo: Found offline by the citation and catalog channels under your scheduler budget. Each row shows its evidence.
Irreversibly deletes the corpus, keys an: Irreversibly deletes the corpus, keys and caches on this machine, then the app must be restarted.
Pick a document on the left.
Keyword extraction splits on spaces and: Keyword extraction splits on spaces and punctuation; it does NOT segment Chinese, Japanese or Korean, so the CJK keyword aggregates shown here are unreliable.
Radial map: the seed keyword at the cent: Radial map: the seed keyword at the centre, its strongest relatives outward.
Text only — images, audio and video aren: Text only — images, audio and video aren't analysed.

not enough data for a time range: not enough data for a time range
No mapped mentions in your corpus yet.
These places have no known coordinate, s: These places have no known coordinate, so they are not drawn on the map.
A place name extracted from article text: A place name extracted from article text, placed at a gazetteer coordinate.
Show the places your corpus MENTIONS, de: Show the places your corpus MENTIONS, deduced from article text (never confirmed). Marker area scales with how many articles mention each place — raw counts, no score.
Established stage: enough breadth and ti: Established stage: enough breadth and time for patterns to be more than a first hint — still descriptive, never a verdict.
Developing stage: patterns are forming b: Developing stage: patterns are forming but the corpus is not yet broad or old enough to lean on heavily.
Stages are descriptive, from real corpus: Stages are descriptive, from real corpus facts — never a score.
Thresholds: early is below {a} articles: Thresholds: early is below {a} articles or {d} days; established is at least {b} articles and {e} days; developing is in between.
Associated keywords for this corpus as a: Associated keywords for this corpus as a radial mind-map (centre → arms → outward). Font size = shared-article volume; PMI-ranked relatives, never causation.
How outlets covering this corpus split i: How outlets covering this corpus split in tone — VADER compound per outlet, with article counts. VADER is an ENGLISH-lexicon method, so tone for non-English coverage is unreliable or absent. No composite score; read the linked articles and attribute for yourself.
Surface variants are merged into entity: Surface variants are merged into entity families.
No keywords indexed for this corpus yet.
Click a row to open that keyword as its: Click a row to open that keyword as its own corpus.
Keywords that co-occur with this corpus,: Keywords that co-occur with this corpus, ranked. Real per-keyword counts — no composite score.
Distinct articles in this corpus that me: Distinct articles in this corpus that mention BOTH the corpus term and this keyword. A real count, never a score.
Distinct articles across the whole corpu: Distinct articles across the whole corpus that mention this keyword. A real count, never a score.
Pointwise mutual information with the co: Pointwise mutual information with the corpus term — association strength, not causation. PMI on small samples is noisy; read it beside the counts.
The keywords that define this corpus as: The keywords that define this corpus as a ranked TABLE — distinct-article spread, total mentions, and PMI association strength to the corpus term. Real counts per keyword, no composite score; PMI on small samples is noisy. Click a row to open that keyword as its own corpus.
No sources for this corpus yet.
Open this source's profile (measured dim: Open this source's profile (measured dimensions, no composite score).
The distinct sources behind this corpus,: The distinct sources behind this corpus, with their per-corpus article count. Country, region, language and tags are stated by the source catalog (asserted, not deduced from text); article counts are exact. No score, no ranking — coverage, not credibility.
Which sources feed this corpus — the dis: Which sources feed this corpus — the distinct outlets behind the member articles, with their per-corpus article count and the catalog metadata they assert (domain, country, region, language, tags). Counts are exact; the country/region/language/tags are stated by the source catalog (not deduced from text). No score, no ranking — descriptive provenance, not credibility.
Only one source in this corpus — nothing: Only one source in this corpus — nothing to compare.
How many articles in this corpus come fr: How many articles in this corpus come from this source — an exact count, never a score.
Mean VADER tone for this source's covera: Mean VADER tone for this source's coverage, with the label. VADER is an ENGLISH-lexicon method — tone for non-English coverage is unreliable or absent. A real value, never a verdict.
The first → last publication date for th: The first → last publication date for this source's coverage in the corpus — real dates, never a score.
This outlet's most distinctive terms whe: This outlet's most distinctive terms when covering the concept (from framing). Descriptive emphasis, not a judgement.
Descriptive comparison — how these sourc: Descriptive comparison — how these sources DIFFER, never a ranking or a credibility judgement. Rows are ordered by volume only (most-covering first); there is no winner and no composite score.
How each source APPROACHES this concept,: How each source APPROACHES this concept, side by side: volume (exact article count), tone (VADER mean + label), timing (first→last publication span) and the outlet's distinctive emphasised terms. A microscope on divergence, not a verdict — no source is ranked above another, no quality is judged, no composite score is computed.

How each source APPROACHES this concept, side by side — volume (exact article count), tone (VADER mean + label), timing (first→last publication span), and that outlet's distinctive emphasised terms. A DESCRIPTIVE comparison of how sources DIFFER, never a ranking, a winner or a credibility judgement: no composite score. Tone is VADER (English-lexicon, unreliable for non-English coverage). With a single source there is nothing to compare.
Open this in the analysis window — its c: Open this in the analysis window — its corpus coverage (co-occurrence in your corpus, never causation)
Transparency: show how every figure was: Transparency: show how every figure was computed and what it does not mean.
Import local .eml newsletter files into: Import local .eml newsletter files into your corpus — searched, analysed and correlated like any article, and kept as a separate, filterable source so email and web never blur.
Anonymised at import, zero network: the: Anonymised at import, zero network: the recipient is never stored, recipient echoes are redacted, tracker tokens are stripped and server-side tracker wrappers are flagged. Nothing is ever fetched — a tracking pixel or link is never followed, so importing can never reveal that you opened or clicked.
These articles join your no-recovery enc: These articles join your no-recovery encrypted corpus. Keep your original .eml files as the re-import path.
Choose one or more .eml files first.
Open in analysis — explore this event in: Open in analysis — explore this event in your corpus
Inline sizes are estimates — the exact s: Inline sizes are estimates — the exact size is read on download.
Most of what you've collected recently c: Most of what you've collected recently comes from just a few sources. This Lead shows you that share, so you can decide whether your reading mix is what you want it to be.
More than half of your recent collection: More than half of your recent collection comes from one single country or language. That can quietly narrow what you see — this Lead points it out so you can balance your sources if you want to.
One of your sources is publishing far fa: One of your sources is publishing far faster than the others — so fast that it's worth asking how. Big newsrooms and wire agencies can be this fast legitimately; this Lead only raises the question.
Only one of your sources carried this st: Only one of your sources carried this story, and nobody else repeated it. That can mean an exclusive worth a look — or just a minor item. The Lead makes no judgement; you decide.
Threshold for this Lead: one origin at o: Threshold for this Lead: one origin at or above 60%
Absolute floor before this Lead can appe: Absolute floor before this Lead can appear
Space-time scenario Leads that watch you: Space-time scenario Leads that watch your own corpus (all local, no network) and offer a one-click investigation view. Untick to switch one off.
Tabs opened from Leads and readers are s: Tabs opened from Leads and readers are suffixed “· FOOS” — Free Open OmniScience, the alpha working name.
Each Lead is one measured signal with it: Each Lead is one measured signal with its evidence and caveat, never a verdict.
Transparency: show how every figure was: Transparency: show how every figure was computed. The caveat is always shown on each Lead.
Early stage: the Leads rest on thin evid: Early stage: the Leads rest on thin evidence — read them as first hints, not established patterns.
reliability_score is operator-set proven: reliability_score is operator-set provenance metadata you assert per source. The app never computes, defaults, or derives it from article data, and never builds a Lead or score from it.
Open in analysis — the article selection: Open in analysis — the article selection behind this Lead
Showing the exact article set behind thi: Showing the exact article set behind this Lead.
No rising keywords in this window yet.
Airplane mode here controls only this ap: Airplane mode here controls only this app's network — it does not switch off your device's network or stop other apps. A userspace app can never equal a hardware switch.
No convergences yet. A cluster needs at: No convergences yet. A cluster needs at least {a} articles AND at least {s} sources sharing a place within the window.
⚠ {n} shared-origin links — sources may: ⚠ {n} shared-origin links — sources may echo one citation, not independent confirmation.
Delete this download and its file?: Delete this download and its file?
Download this offline map region? These: Download this offline map region? These can be very large (several GB to tens of GB).
Deduced from {n} articles ({s} sources),: Deduced from {n} articles ({s} sources), never confirmed.
A date your articles mention — deduced f: A date your articles mention — deduced from text, never confirmed.
Ollama isn't running. Start it (or insta: Ollama isn't running. Start it (or install it) to use the LLM features; once running, your installed models appear here.
No models installed yet — pull one below: No models installed yet — pull one below.
Hardware fit is advisory — it informs yo: Hardware fit is advisory — it informs your choice, it doesn't decide. Pull any model from the full library below.
Could not set the active model:: Could not set the active model:
Remove this model and free its disk spac: Remove this model and free its disk space?
Pull a local model (downloads over the c: Pull a local model (downloads over the clear internet via Ollama)
Could not load the statistics directory.
Register all official-statistics produce: Register all official-statistics producers as DISABLED sources? They are added controversial and NOT scraped.
Download rate is steered by concurrency:: Download rate is steered by concurrency: more parallel hosts means higher throughput, up to a safe ceiling. The app measures its own real download rate and raises or lowers the number of concurrent hosts to track your target, backing off automatically when CPU, memory or the single encrypted writer become the limit (see the collection-performance log in the debug bundle). Per-host politeness is never traded for speed.
A target, not a guarantee. The app adjus: A target, not a guarantee. The app adjusts how many sources it fetches at once to approach this rate — always across DIFFERENT sites, never hammering one (robots.txt and per-host limits are respected). Actual speed is limited by the sources, your connection (much lower over Tor), your CPU and the encrypted database. “Maximum” fetches as fast as your machine and the sources allow.
The date-extraction log shows, per artic: The date-extraction log shows, per article, the dates the extractor found versus the date-like text it skipped — the evidence for catching more dates. Counts and bounded samples only; local, never transmitted.
Type a command, or search the corpus…: Type a command, or search the corpus…
Drag to pan · scroll to zoom · drag a no: Drag to pan · scroll to zoom · drag a node to move it · click to open
Every interface shows the same data and: Every interface shows the same data and the same caveats: caveats stay visible, the network-consent step is unchanged, and no interface invents a score.
Pick an interface to try it live. Your c: Pick an interface to try it live. Your corpus, your data and every honesty guarantee stay the same — only the look, layout and interaction change. Switching reloads the app to apply.
The original — dense, honest, fully feat: The original — dense, honest, fully featured.
Calm and uncluttered; depth on demand.
A mission-control dashboard of live tile: A mission-control dashboard of live tiles.
A mobile-first card stream with a bottom: A mobile-first card stream with a bottom bar.
Your briefing, typeset like a publicatio: Your briefing, typeset like a publication.
No coverage to chart for this term yet.
Open the analysis from a keyword or a se: Open the analysis from a keyword or a search to see its combined trend.
Switch to Indexed to overlay commodity p: Switch to Indexed to overlay commodity prices honestly (different units).
each on its own real-unit scale: each on its own real-unit scale
Article counts on a shared time axis. Co: Article counts on a shared time axis. Co-occurrence in your corpus, never causation.
Indexed to 100 at the window start — rel: Indexed to 100 at the window start — relative movement, not absolute levels. Hover shows the real value. Co-occurrence in your corpus, never causation.
Open these articles as a new analysis co: Open these articles as a new analysis corpus
{n} near-identical copies across {m} sou: {n} near-identical copies across {m} sources = effectively one voice
{n} near-identical copies from one sourc: {n} near-identical copies from one source = one voice
No near-identical clusters detected in t: No near-identical clusters detected in this corpus — not proof there is no coordination, only that none was found at this threshold.
articles in this corpus citing the same: articles in this corpus citing the same outbound page
No outbound page is cited by 2+ articles: No outbound page is cited by 2+ articles in this corpus yet.
Open every article citing this origin as: Open every article citing this origin as a new corpus
Several articles citing the same page ar: Several articles citing the same page are not independent confirmation — one origin, several echoes.
Minimal — keep the app folder and your d: Minimal — keep the app folder and your data
Full — also delete the app folder (data: Full — also delete the app folder (data kept)
Secure — also wipe your data and keys: Secure — also wipe your data and keys
Also wipe my data and keys (irreversible: Also wipe my data and keys (irreversible)
One of {n} near-identical copies = effec: One of {n} near-identical copies = effectively one voice. Open Related to inspect the cluster.
{n} of these are near-identical copies —: {n} of these are near-identical copies — fewer independent voices than the count suggests (see Related).
Near-identical copies in your corpus — e: Near-identical copies in your corpus — effectively one voice, not independent confirmation. See Related.
Branch selected into a new corpus →: Branch selected into a new corpus →
Select one or more rows to branch.
No articles in the selected rows.
There are three system prompts — Summary: There are three system prompts — Summary (used for one or many articles), Translation (one or many), and Synthesis (one combined output across several). They instruct the local model, and the exact prompt used is recorded with every result.
How long Ollama keeps the model in memor: How long Ollama keeps the model in memory after a request, so the next one does not reload it. Examples: 30m, 1h, -1 (never unload), 0 (unload now).
Leave a prompt empty to use the built-in: Leave a prompt empty to use the built-in default (shown as the placeholder). In the translation prompt, the placeholder {target} is replaced by the target language.
Summarize every matched article with you: Summarize every matched article with your local model — stored with its model and date, never analysed for keywords.
Translate every matched article with you: Translate every matched article with your local model — stored with its model and date, never analysed for keywords.
Runs a local model over each article — t: Runs a local model over each article — this can take a while. Each result is stored with its model and date; nothing leaves your machine, and keyword analysis is never affected.
Open an article to read its summary or t: Open an article to read its summary or translation.
Coordination is surfaced automatically n: Coordination is surfaced automatically now — as Leads, in the analysis window's Related view, and inline on article lists and the reader. Open the manual desk to collapse a coordinated network to one voice, look up a source profile, or manage web-of-trust annotations.
— save a condition, get a Lead when it m: — save a condition, get a Lead when it matches
A watch is a saved search the app keeps: A watch is a saved search the app keeps an eye on. When your corpus gains enough new articles matching it (your own threshold, within a recent window), it fires a watch Lead card on Home — local-only, no notifications, no network. A watch is a prompt to read, never a verdict or a score.
Run the engine now (it also runs after e: Run the engine now (it also runs after every collection pass)
Pull newsletters live from a mailbox ins: Pull newsletters live from a mailbox instead of exporting files one by one. Each message goes through the same anonymise-at-ingest path as the file import — the recipient is never stored, no raw message is retained, and tracking links are de-toxed and never followed.
It is refused under airplane mode, and c: It is refused under airplane mode, and connects to your mail provider directly over TLS (revealing your IP to that provider, like any email client) — it is not routed through Tor. Credentials are used for this one fetch and not stored.
Sizes are estimates — the exact size is: Sizes are estimates — the exact size is read from the mirror on download.
Download OpenStreetMap region extracts f: Download OpenStreetMap region extracts for offline use — managed like the Wikipedia dumps (resumable, with pause/resume, and a download queue). Regions are listed largest first; the size shown is a dated estimate from a bundled catalogue (no network) — the exact size is read from the mirror when the download starts. Continents come from Geofabrik; the whole planet from the OpenStreetMap planet mirror. These can be very large (a continent is several GB; the planet is tens of GB).
Downloading is a network action: it goes: Downloading is a network action: it goes through this app's one network switch, so it is refused while airplane mode is on and asks for your consent the first time you go online. The download runs as a background job (this app's network only — never your device or other apps).
Fetch a published figure from a document: Fetch a published figure from a documented official machine endpoint (the World Bank API, or a Eurostat / SDMX-JSON dataset). Each value is stored with its full provenance trail and a first-class vintage — a re-fetch later is a new row, never an overwrite, so revisions are preserved as evidence. Producers are compared side by side, never averaged, and a cell mixing different units / seasonal adjustment / base years is flagged, not reconciled. No score is ever computed.
This is the one networked action here: t: This is the one networked action here: the fetch egresses over your configured transport and is refused while airplane mode is on.
Replay due fetches now (also runs in the: Replay due fetches now (also runs in the background while you are online)
Every figure you fetch is tracked and re: Every figure you fetch is tracked and re-fetched on its interval while you are online, storing a new vintage each time (revisions are preserved, never overwritten). Disable or remove any you do not want.
The local language models power Summariz: The local language models power Summarize, Translate and Synthesize. This shows what you actually have installed (live from Ollama) and a small suggested list annotated for your hardware. Pick the active model, pull new ones, or remove them — all from here. Newer and better models appear constantly — pull any tag below, or browse
Pulling a model is a network action: it: Pulling a model is a network action: it downloads through the Ollama process over the clear internet — not through this app's Tor proxy — so it is refused while airplane mode is on, and asks your consent the first time you go online. The download happens on your machine; the model then runs fully offline.
Track watched pages per language edition: Track watched pages per language edition (editions are per-language, not per-country). We store one baseline snapshot per page, then only diffs/deltas of new edits — so cosmetic changes cost almost nothing — and flag large / suspicious edits (size delta, revert/blank tags, anonymous, bursts, optional ORES). Candidates are surfaced for you to judge; nothing is labelled "disinformation".
Optional, heavy offline full-text baseli: Optional, heavy offline full-text baselines (per-language dumps) are configured further down in this panel — pick an edition from the list and download it. Not needed for the change-tracking above.
Optional, heavy, and separate from live: Optional, heavy, and separate from live change-tracking. Pick a language edition below and download the official Wikimedia current-text dump (resumable, with pause/resume). Editions are listed flat — your languages first, then the largest editions; the real download size is read from the server when you click Estimate size — nothing is guessed. enwiki ≈ tens of GB; most editions are far smaller. Baselines are only for offline reading/search — the change-tracking above does not need them.
The list is limited to the app's languag: The list is limited to the app's languages — the editions this app can read and analyze. Type in the box to filter by name, autonym or code. Each edition shows a coarse size tier and an estimated download size inline, while Estimate size reports the exact current dump size from the server. You can also type any edition code that isn't listed.
Local LLM — click to open AI settings: Local LLM — click to open AI settings
Ollama models live outside your corpus,: Ollama models live outside your corpus, so they are NOT in the full backup. Back them up separately to avoid re-downloading (multi-GB; slow over Tor). Restore is additive and bit-identical — existing blobs are skipped, never overwritten.
Tamper-evident, signed provenance for le: Tamper-evident, signed provenance for legal defensibility. Toggles below are preferences; the status line shows what this build can actually do.
Add an ML-DSA (FIPS 204) signature along: Add an ML-DSA (FIPS 204) signature alongside Ed25519 so the chain stays verifiable against a future quantum adversary.
Anchor entries into Bitcoin via public c: Anchor entries into Bitcoin via public calendars. Independent proof of time, but requires network egress and reveals your IP/timing to the calendars.
Privacy: OpenTimestamps publishes only a: Privacy: OpenTimestamps publishes only a hash, but submitting it reveals your IP and timing to the calendar operators. For an anonymous source, route traffic through Tor (HTTPS_PROXY) or stay local-only.
Irreversibly deletes the corpus, keys an: Irreversibly deletes the corpus, keys and caches on this machine, then the app must be restarted. This cannot be undone. Overwrite-in-place does not guarantee unrecoverability on SSD/flash — for that, use full-disk encryption (LUKS/Qubes/Tails) and destroy the key.
The virtualenv and desktop launchers are: The virtualenv and desktop launchers are always removed; whether the app folder and your data go too depends on the mode. You can also run it in a terminal:
Recursive crawl stays inside each source: Recursive crawl stays inside each source's own domain, honours robots.txt (fail-closed), is rate-limited, and is bounded by the depth / page caps above — it discovers articles, it does not mirror sites.
Select several sources and fetch all the: Select several sources and fetch all their feeds in one run. Filter by language, country or type, then tick the ones you want. Each is fetched ethically (robots fail-closed, rate-limited); up to 50 per run.
Order is re-randomised every pass — stra: Order is re-randomised every pass — stratified by language and tag, not a fixed queue.
Each country is shaded by how many of yo: Each country is shaded by how many of your sources are based there — darker means more. A country with no located source stays un-shaded — honestly “no data”, never zero. Small territories the coarse outline can’t draw appear as points, and sources with no country are counted but never placed on the map.
sources have no country and are not show: sources have no country and are not shown on the map.
No located sources yet — add sources wit: No located sources yet — add sources with a country, or collect some articles.
with no country — counted, not mapped.
Catalogued sources based in each country: Catalogued sources based in each country — counts only, no score.
Articles collected from sources in each: Articles collected from sources in each country — counts only, no score.
Keyword mentions in articles from source: Keyword mentions in articles from sources in each country — counts only, no score.
Mean article tone (VADER) — English-lexi: Mean article tone (VADER) — English-lexicon only, unreliable for other languages; only English articles are scored. Deduced, never a verdict.
Mentioned places: deduced from text, nev: Mentioned places: deduced from text, never confirmed.
Online — click to go offline (airplane m: Online — click to go offline (airplane mode); every new network request will be refused.
Offline (airplane mode) — click to go on: Offline (airplane mode) — click to go online; you'll be asked to confirm first.
PANIC WIPE: irreversibly delete the corp: PANIC WIPE: irreversibly delete the corpus, keys and caches on this machine?
This cannot be undone. Type-confirm foll: This cannot be undone. Type-confirm follows.
To confirm, type WIPE in capitals:: To confirm, type WIPE in capitals:
Open this graph fullscreen with scale co: Open this graph fullscreen with scale controls
Open this in the analysis window — its c: Open this in the analysis window — its corpus coverage
Open this in the analysis window: Open this in the analysis window
Correlate price change vs news volume: Correlate price change vs news volume
No coverage recorded for this country ye: No coverage recorded for this country yet.
Mean tone uses the English-only VADER le: Mean tone uses the English-only VADER lexicon; non-English articles are not scored.
Bounded preview from a downloaded .osm.p: Bounded preview from a downloaded .osm.pbf — not the full region; no network.
No offline-map region downloaded yet — g: No offline-map region downloaded yet — get one in Settings → Offline map.
Could not read the downloaded region.
No completed sessions yet — each finishe: No completed sessions yet — each finished collection pass (a session while online) appears here with its result.
Airplane mode — engaged. Click to go onl: Airplane mode — engaged. Click to go online in the app (you'll be asked to confirm).
Online. Click to go offline (airplane mo: Online. Click to go offline (airplane mode) — stops all collection.
Import sources from a CSV file: Import sources from a CSV file
A Markdown document recording the verbat: A Markdown document recording the verbatim query, app version and per-article provenance — for verdicts and peer review. Records selection only; concludes nothing.
Disable (never delete) every enabled sou: Disable (never delete) every enabled source in a language the keyword engine cannot manage. Reversible from the sources table.
One local-LLM synthesis across the top r: One local-LLM synthesis across the top results (max 20) — shared facts, disagreements, open questions, citing article numbers. Assistance, never a verdict.
Tell the local model what to extract. Ou: Tell the local model what to extract. Output one item per line — each becomes a piece of metadata of the type above.
Found offline by the citation and catalo: Found offline by the citation and catalog channels under your scheduler budget. Each row shows its evidence. Promote creates a disabled source you still have to enable; Dismiss is remembered and never re-suggested.
The keyword engine can only analyse lang: The keyword engine can only analyse languages with a stoplist and word-segmentation. Sources in other languages (no stoplist, or zh/ja which aren't segmented) produce junk keywords that skew analytics and slow the app. They're kept and re-enablable — disabling them just stops gathering material we'd mangle until a stoplist for that language lands.
Restore is additive-only — the destructi: Restore is additive-only — the destructive replace-restore was removed. Restore through the merge restore above (the signed backup artifact), which complements your corpus and never overwrites it.
Which external links or domains many of: Which external links or domains many of your articles point to — a citation-graph signal grounded in what reporters actually reference. Click a row to assemble every article in your corpus that cites it (opens the offline stored copy).
Surface variants are grouped automatical: Surface variants are grouped automatically (Trump = Trump's = Donald Trump). Here you correct it: tick two or more rows and Merge them, or click a member's ✕ to split it out. Your decisions win over the automatic rules and are fully reversible.
Gather several families — or cross-langu: Gather several families — or cross-language rings (one concept across languages, e.g. election / élection / wahl) — under one theme (e.g. Russia–Ukraine war over Russia, Ukraine, Putin, sanctions…) for sorting and discovery. A ring member makes the super-group span languages (the super-ring model). Pure curation — nothing in the keyword store changes.
Clusters of articles that converge on th: Clusters of articles that converge on the same place within a time window, on the date mentioned in the text. Independence is measured by distinct sources, not article count. Co-occurrence is never causation — a prompt to read, not proof anything happened. Click a cluster to assemble its exact article set.
AI-generated tentative translation — unr: AI-generated tentative translation — unreliable, not verified.
Local AI is offline — start Ollama (and: Local AI is offline — start Ollama (and turn airplane mode off) for tentative translations.
Label: Label
Summarize this article with the local mo: Summarize this article with the local model — stored, labelled AI-derived, never the keyword index.
Translate this article into the interfac: Translate this article into the interface language with the local model.
Summarize / Translate run a local model: Summarize / Translate run a local model per article — results are stored, labelled AI-derived, and never touch the keyword index.
AI summary — unreliable, verify against: AI summary — unreliable, verify against the source.
AI translation — unreliable, verify agai: AI translation — unreliable, verify against the source.
Activity & vitals — click to open the ta: Activity & vitals — click to open the task manager
Disable sources in languages we can't an: Disable sources in languages we can't analyse yet
Older backup tool: download a raw .db sn: Older backup tool: download a raw .db snapshot…
Your draft — Leads you pinned to investi: Your draft — Leads you pinned to investigate together
An encrypted backup is almost exactly th: An encrypted backup is almost exactly the same file size as a plaintext one — AES-256-GCM adds only a fixed ~64 bytes of overhead, so the size matching is expected, not a sign it isn't encrypted. You can confirm any backup is genuine ciphertext in the Restore preview below (it reports “encrypted (AES-256-GCM)”).
Browse keywords by their type/topic tags: Browse keywords by their type/topic tags, hide noise, and apply the curated baseline tags to your existing corpus. Tags are labelled assertions, never scores; hiding is reversible (see Keyword filtering under General). Nothing in the keyword store is rewritten.
Each country is shaded by how many of yo: Each country is shaded by how many of your sources are based there — darker means more. A country with no located source stays un-shaded — honestly “no data”, never zero. Small territories the coarse outline can’t draw appear as points, and sources with no country are counted but never placed on the map. Use the in-map controls to change what’s measured (sources · articles · keywords · tone), group by continent, overlay the places your corpus mentions, or turn on the time signals and sweep the moment in focus.
Each pinned Lead exports with its source: Each pinned Lead exports with its source links, method and caveat — reproducible by design. For a signed, tamper-evident copy of the underlying articles, use Evidence & custody.
Leave a prompt empty to use the built-in: Leave a prompt empty to use the built-in default (shown as the placeholder). In the translation prompt, the placeholder {target} is replaced by the target language; in the keyword-extraction prompt, {max_terms} is the per-article limit.
Most users won't need this. Curated feed: Most users won't need this. Curated feeds give trends out of the box; extraction rules let you add a price from a specific page (a number is recorded only where your CSS selector lands on one — never a guess).
Real series from official feeds (FRED →: Real series from official feeds (FRED → World Bank / EIA): FX (EUR/USD), energy (WTI, Brent, gas), metals & gold. Every point comes straight from the downloaded file — nothing scraped or guessed.
Run one of your custom AI extractors ove: Run one of your custom AI extractors over these articles — results are stored as AI-derived metadata of that type, labelled unreliable, never the trusted keyword index.
The keyword self-test challenges the key: The keyword self-test challenges the keyword pre-selection on curated cases — most importantly that the organisation "WHO" stays distinct from the pronoun "who", plus the per-language tweaks (German nouns stay terms, sentence-initial capitals aren't entities, the stopword/weekday batches, the singular/plural family merge, cross-language equivalence rings, and the curated baseline tags). It runs the real pipeline and reports pass/fail per case — share it so a regression is caught early. No corpus content, no network, no scores.
The keyword-engine report measures how t: The keyword-engine report measures how the engine is doing over your real corpus: composition, entity precision, cross-language translation coverage (how many top keywords have a ring), tag coverage, which languages are fully functional vs degraded (zh/ja aren't segmented), the self-test result, and indicative extraction/query timings. Counts and measured timings only — never a quality score. Send two over time and the difference shows whether an optimization landed.
There are four system prompts — Summary: There are four system prompts — Summary (used for one or many articles), Translation (one or many), Synthesis (one combined output across several), and Keyword extraction (the built-in keyword/entity indexer). They instruct the local model, and the exact prompt used is recorded with every result.
Your own AI extractors — each a prompt t: Your own AI extractors — each a prompt that pulls one kind of rich metadata from an article (an extension of the built-in who / where / when). Output items are stored as AI-derived metadata of the type you name, shown beside the article and labelled "AI-derived · unreliable", never mixed into the trusted keyword index. Define and manage them here; run them over a selection from the search toolbar or an analysis window (the "Run extractor" action), or automatically on new articles via each extractor's run-on-ingest toggle.
A curated directory of government and in: A curated directory of government and international statistical agencies — deliberately global (BRICS, Africa and smaller economies alongside the usual Western producers and IGOs). Every producer here is a stanced source: an official figure carries its producing state's interests — this directory shows WHO publishes so you can judge for yourself. We attach no credibility verdict and no score. No figures are fetched and nothing leaves your machine.
Coordinated near-duplicate floods are sh: Coordinated near-duplicate floods are shown by default (equal but aware) — never silently collapsed. Apply a collapse to count a network as one voice; it stays flagged and expandable, and reverting reproduces the raw equal counts exactly.
Protected mode cannot guarantee anonymit: Protected mode cannot guarantee anonymity — you must run and trust the proxy yourself. SOCKS proxies need the optional [safety] extra.
Live counts from the unified store. Ever: Live counts from the unified store. Every figure is a real COUNT(*) or on-disk byte size — nothing is estimated.
Analytics over keywords and entities ext: Analytics over keywords and entities extracted from ingested article text (people, companies and places are single units). Trends, associations (PMI) and context are real aggregates with sample sizes — nothing is invented.
Off by default; everything else works wi: Off by default; everything else works without it. (Discovering RSS feeds for sources you added yourself stays local-path and is not affected.)
The app measures structure — coordinatio: The app measures structure — coordination, novelty, output capacity, transparency — and you decide. There is deliberately no trust score: a single number over incommensurable dimensions would bake in bias and silence small, foreign, new or dissident sources. Surface, never suppress.
Annotations are descriptive, contestable: Annotations are descriptive, contestable facts (ownership, leaning, coordination, corrections) — never a score. Publish yours as a signed bundle; import the ones you choose to trust. Aggregation shows who asserted what; dissent is shown, not averaged.
Stored in your encrypted corpus database: Stored in your encrypted corpus database (app_state) — your settings ride your backups; no telemetry, nothing leaves the machine.
Restore is additive-only: use the merge: Restore is additive-only: use the merge restore above — it complements your corpus and never replaces it. (The destructive replace-restore was removed; a raw .db download is a snapshot for manual/advanced use.)
\"Dumb\" keywords (function words like t: "Dumb" keywords (function words like the, you, not, one) are removed automatically by a built-in multilingual stoplist. Fine-tune below: exclude extra terms (one per line or comma-separated), set the minimum length, and toggle options. Excluding hides a keyword everywhere (reversibly — stored mentions are kept). You can also click the ✕ next to any keyword in Insights.
The virtualenv and desktop launchers are: The virtualenv and desktop launchers are always removed; whether the app folder and your data go too depends on the mode. You can also run it in a terminal: ./install.sh --uninstall
The app is shutting down — you can close: The app is shutting down — you can close this tab.
Open Omniscience has been uninstalled an: Open Omniscience has been uninstalled and the app has stopped. You can close this window.
Shut down the app? The server will stop: Shut down the app? The server will stop and you'll need to relaunch it. Your data is untouched.
Enter a folder path on this machine.
Enter a folder to restore from.
Re-import the cleaned files to replace t: Re-import the cleaned files to replace them.
Wikipedia dumps, offline maps and LLM mo: Wikipedia dumps, offline maps and LLM models are large, public, re-downloadable files — too big for the encrypted full backup. Copy them server-side into a folder you choose on this machine (e.g. an external drive's mount path). They are copied as-is (not encrypted — that is what makes 100 GB feasible); your private corpus stays in the encrypted full backup. A second run re-copies nothing unchanged, restore is additive (a file you already have is never overwritten), and in-progress downloads are skipped.
Restore large data from a folder: Restore large data from a folder
Copy Wikipedia dumps, maps and models BA: Copy Wikipedia dumps, maps and models BACK from a folder backup into this machine, additively — a file you already have is kept, never overwritten.
Import a whole folder (for very large se: Import a whole folder (for very large sets)
Point to a folder ON THIS MACHINE and ev: Point to a folder ON THIS MACHINE and every .eml under it is imported as a pausable background job — for a 20 GB+ archive the file picker can't handle. Same anonymise-at-ingest path, zero network. Already-imported messages are skipped, so you can pause and resume safely.
Delete every imported-newsletter article: Delete every imported-newsletter article (.eml + mailbox) from your corpus — for replacing a faulty set with a cleaned re-import. Restoring a backup is additive, so leaving newsletters out of a backup never removes them from the live corpus; this does.
This cannot be undone except from a back: This cannot be undone except from a backup.
It removes the articles and all their an: It removes the articles and all their analysis (keywords, dates, places); the empty newsletter source stays so a clean re-import re-attaches.
— large files: use the “Large data backu: — large files: use the “Large data backup (folder / external drive)” panel above
— restore these from the “Large data bac: — restore these from the “Large data backup” panel above
— untick to back up without them (e.g. t: — untick to back up without them (e.g. to re-import fixed ones)
— untick to restore everything except th: — untick to restore everything except them
— use the separate “Local LLM models” ba: — use the separate “Local LLM models” backup above
— use the separate “Local LLM models” re: — use the separate “Local LLM models” restore above
The preview reflects your choice — what: The preview reflects your choice — what you preview is exactly what gets merged.
Shut down the app (stops the server — yo: Shut down the app (stops the server — your data is untouched)
Re-indexing recomputes keywords, dates/p: Re-indexing recomputes keywords, dates/places/entities and sentiment for every article with the current engine — use it after an upgrade to refresh older articles (for example to drain stale CSS/markup keywords from newsletters imported before the markup-stripping fix). Heavy on a large corpus; it runs in the background batch by batch. Summaries and translations are left untouched.
Synthesize a chosen set of these article: Synthesize a chosen set of these articles with your local model — shared facts, disagreements, open questions, citing article numbers. You pick the members; assistance, never a verdict.
The scaling benchmark times the heavy re: The scaling benchmark times the heavy read paths against your real corpus, several runs each, and reports cold vs warm timings per case — so we can check that big corpora stay fast on modest hardware. It records your corpus size, the keyword-counter freshness and the engine mode alongside the numbers so the results are interpretable. Read-only and local; nothing is transmitted.
Synthesize a chosen set of your results: Synthesize a chosen set of your results with your local model — shared facts, disagreements, open questions, citing article numbers. You pick the members; assistance, never a verdict.
Pruning removes keywords that no view re: Pruning removes keywords that no view references any more — terms left with zero mentions after a re-index drains markup junk or a merge. Pure cleanup that shrinks the keyword count; a keyword with any mention, and any term you curated into a family or group, is never touched. Run it after re-indexing for the biggest effect.
Re-index every article with the current: Re-index every article with the current engine, then remove the keywords left with no mentions — the recommended order in one action.
No country data yet — use “Load standard: No country data yet — use “Load standard country data” (online) to fetch it from the World Bank.
No country data yet — use the Countries: No country data yet — use the Countries tab to load it (online).
Per-country official statistics (World B: Per-country official statistics (World Bank): GDP, population, life expectancy, labour, public finance + common indices. Each value is a producer's published figure, never a credibility score; a missing value is a published gap, not zero; producers are shown, never averaged.
Any: a source with ANY chosen tag. All:: Any: a source with ANY chosen tag. All: a source with EVERY chosen tag.
Open this Lead's corpus in a new window: Open this Lead's corpus in a new window
Restore from a volume backup folder: Restore from a volume backup folder
Source-catalogue reach (how many countri: Source-catalogue reach (how many countries the catalog spans, scored against ISO 3166):
A world map of how many ARTICLES you hav: A world map of how many ARTICLES you have collected per country (by each source's catalogued country). Measured from the data; refreshes live.
Everything you've DOWNLOADED and everyth: Everything you've DOWNLOADED and everything the app has EXTRAPOLATED from it — real counts and on-disk sizes, never an estimate.
drag to pan · hover a dot for details ·: drag to pan · hover a dot for details · zoom in to read city names
Verify + repair (from parity) + reassemb: Verify + repair (from parity) + reassemble the volume set, then merge ADDITIVELY into your corpus — nothing is replaced or deleted.
For every Home card (Lead): does clickin: For every Home card (Lead): does clicking it open its EXACT corpus (hard-linked) or a fuzzy text search that loses it (search-fallback)? Flags the mismatches.
Every diagnostics log in one archive — o: Every diagnostics log in one archive — one click instead of nine. Carries the bounded keyword-log digest (the full keyword dump has its own 'All keywords' button).
Every keyword in the corpus (not just th: Every keyword in the corpus (not just the top 5000 per language), in one sizeable archive — the ~20 MB cap still applies; if hit, the manifest says has_more and you grab &page=2.
Plot cumulative distinct keywords agains: Plot cumulative distinct keywords against cumulative words added. A curve that bends over = the vocabulary is saturating (healthy); a near-straight line (Heaps β ≈ 1) = new keywords are still minted for almost every word (markup/code/unsegmented junk).
Flag a stored figure whose newest vintag: Flag a stored figure whose newest vintage moved a past value unusually far for that series' own revision history — retrospective, no score.
History must not be silently rewritten. This flags an official figure whose most recent vintage revised a past value by an amount unusually large for that series' own revision history — retrospective only (never a forecast), naming the shape (an outlier-sized revision), never the intent. Leave the series id above blank to scan all stored figures.
Plot the series over time. A new line se: Plot the series over time. A new line segment starts at every unit / base-year / seasonal-adjustment change (never joined); a gap is a break, never interpolated.
Going offline — cutting new network requ: Going offline — cutting new network requests…
This single-file download is capped at a: This single-file download is capped at about 2 GiB. For a larger corpus use the Export / Back up… flow (Settings → Data & backup), which streams to a folder with no size limit.
The corpus is written as encrypted volum: The corpus is written as encrypted volumes plus parity. Parity (corruption recovery) requires the analysis extra (numpy); otherwise the backup is volumes only.
What do you want to back up?: What do you want to back up?
Could not load the inventory — see conso: Could not load the inventory — see console
Enter a passphrase for the encrypted cor: Enter a passphrase for the encrypted corpus.
encrypted volumes — additive, nothing yo: encrypted volumes — additive, nothing you already have is overwritten
Source CSV found — import it from the So: Source CSV found — import it from the Sources panel for now.
Legacy single-file backup found — restor: Legacy single-file backup found — restore via the old panel (being retired).
Nothing importable found in this folder.
What do you want to import?: What do you want to import?
Enter the passphrase to restore the corp: Enter the passphrase to restore the corpus.
Your corpus is unlocked. Getting it read: Your corpus is unlocked. Getting it ready — this can take a moment on a large database, and only happens once per start.
Rebind the app's global shortcuts. Chang: Rebind the app's global shortcuts. Changes are saved on this device only and never transmitted. The command palette is bound by default; the others are opt-in.
These contextual keys are always availab: These contextual keys are always available and are not rebindable.
Pick a cross-language ring — one concept: Pick a cross-language ring — one concept counted across every language — to see where its coverage comes from, split by the producing source's country and language. Counts only; a source with an unknown country is shown honestly (never mapped, never guessed).
No cross-country coverage for this conce: No cross-country coverage for this concept yet.
No located sources for this concept yet.
Newest first by the article's published: Newest first by the article's published date (set by the publisher) among sources tagged this way. Every title opens the offline stored copy.
No articles for this tag yet.
Compress antiquity so recent years — whe: Compress antiquity so recent years — where most events are — get more of the slider
No editions indexed yet — pick one above: No editions indexed yet — pick one above and Build index.
Pick a downloaded edition to index.
Clear the full-text index for {w}? It is: Clear the full-text index for {w}? It is rebuildable from the local dump.
No full-text index yet — pick a download: No full-text index yet — pick a downloaded edition above and Build index first.
The query could not be parsed — try plai: The query could not be parsed — try plain words or AND/OR/NOT.
No matches in your indexed dump bodies.
Search the BODIES of your downloaded dum: Search the BODIES of your downloaded dumps, not just their titles. An edition must be indexed first — a one-time local sweep; the index is a side-file beside the dumps, rides no backup, and is rebuildable at any time. Matches are a snapshot as of the dump date, not the live article. Local, zero network.
Words or a Boolean query (AND OR NOT)…: Words or a Boolean query (AND OR NOT)…
Full-text search runs over the index bui: Full-text search runs over the index built above — the same Boolean syntax and ranking as article search. A result opens in the local reader above (its raw wikitext, as of the dump date).
Editions with a complete multistream dum: Editions with a complete multistream dump on disk
Restrict the search to one indexed editi: Restrict the search to one indexed edition
No local hazard snapshot — silence is no: No local hazard snapshot — silence is not safety.
Newest first by collection time (never t: Newest first by collection time (never the publisher's claimed date). Set the substance gates; each article shows its real word count and cited-source count.
No recent articles pass these gates yet: No recent articles pass these gates yet — loosen the gates or collect more.
An asserted content channel (newsletter,: An asserted content channel (newsletter, web article, wiki, statistic, law, market, discovery), never a quality score. Click a channel to explore its corpus.
Not in your corpus yet — no stats.
No articles for this channel yet.
The exact text of this revision is store: The exact text of this revision is stored on this machine.
No tracked revisions stored for this pag: No tracked revisions stored for this page yet.
No flagged tracked revisions stored for: No flagged tracked revisions stored for this page yet.
No stored diff (no parent, or tracked wi: No stored diff (no parent, or tracked without diffs).
The tracked slice of edits stored on thi: The tracked slice of edits stored on this machine, newest first — not necessarily every historical revision. Each diff is the compact added / removed summary captured when the edit was tracked (truncated per side), not a live re-diff. Counts only, no score.
See this page's tracked revision history: See this page's tracked revision history — the stored edits, newest first, with each diff.
Does a keyword's daily coverage line up: Does a keyword's daily coverage line up with the moon's illuminated fraction? This screens the most-mentioned keywords with a circular-shift permutation test and corrects the whole family with Benjamini-Hochberg FDR, so a survivor beat multiple testing — never a bare significant p. Correlation is not causation; the common, honest outcome is that nothing survives. Counts and statistics only, no score.
Screening the corpus… a permutation test: Screening the corpus… a permutation test runs per keyword, so this can take a few seconds.
No series survived the multiple-testing: No series survived the multiple-testing correction — the honest, expected result.
No series had enough active days to test: No series had enough active days to test.
A single test, not corrected for multipl: A single test, not corrected for multiple comparisons — screen many keywords for an honest, FDR-corrected result.
Too few active days to test this keyword: Too few active days to test this keyword honestly.
Countries shaded by a measured dimension: Countries shaded by a measured dimension of your corpus — no data stays un-shaded, never zero. No score.
Location-based events extracted from you: Location-based events extracted from your corpus, by type. Deduced, never a verdict — click a place to open its corpus.
The places your articles mention — what: The places your articles mention — what the corpus is about. Deduced from text, never confirmed.
Where the servers we reached are located: Where the servers we reached are located (offline geo) — a CDN edge / anycast host, not the publisher's origin; unavailable over Tor.
No located events in your corpus yet — c: No located events in your corpus yet — collect more, or explore the Coverage lens.
Story types are deduced from your corpus: Story types are deduced from your corpus by event kind — counts only, never a verdict or ranking.
Each lens presets the map; the in-map co: Each lens presets the map; the in-map controls still fine-tune what’s measured, group by continent, and combine layers. Per-country statistical data (GDP, population, life expectancy…) lives in the Governments tab. Small territories the coarse outline can’t draw appear as points; data with no country is counted but never placed on the map.
Who paid for or commissioned it: Who paid for or commissioned it
When it was in the field: When it was in the field
Missing core disclosures — needed to int: Missing core disclosures — needed to interpret this poll at all:
The exact question, shown verbatim as st: The exact question, shown verbatim as structure:
Core disclosures — the floor to interpre: Core disclosures — the floor to interpret a poll
Advanced: paste disclosed fields as JSON: Advanced: paste disclosed fields as JSON
Any keys here override the fields above: Any keys here override the fields above (and extra keys are allowed) — for a disclosure the form has no box for.
Enter what a poll DISCLOSED and get a pe: Enter what a poll DISCLOSED and get a per-item checklist of what was stated versus not — the disclosure FLOOR, never a score. It records PRESENCE only (a disclosed n=100 counts like n=10000), non-disclosure of a core item outranks any disclosed imperfection, and it never grades, ranks, or calls a poll “useless”. It surfaces the facts and lets you conclude.
Records, per standard methodological dis: Records, per standard methodological disclosure, only whether it was STATED — never whether the value is good. No composite score, no ranking, never calls a poll “useless”.
Choose a folder to restore from.
This folder is not writable — pick anoth: This folder is not writable — pick another for a backup destination.
(volumes only — parity needs the analysi: (volumes only — parity needs the analysis features)
Import continues in the background — wat: Import continues in the background — watch it in the task manager.
Destination folder (on this machine / a: Destination folder (on this machine / a mounted drive)
Passphrase — encrypts the corpus (no rec: Passphrase — encrypts the corpus (no recovery for a lost passphrase)
Loading country data in the background…: Loading country data in the background…
Applying baseline tags in the background: Applying baseline tags in the background…
The app is busy — retrying shortly…: The app is busy — retrying shortly…
Decrypted copy on disk — remove it delib: Decrypted copy on disk — remove it deliberately.
After an update, the first unlock may ru: After an update, the first unlock may run one-time migrations and rebuild indexes — this can take several minutes on a large corpus, and happens only once.
Fetch the standard country indicators fo: Fetch the standard country indicators for every country from the World Bank (online, asks for consent first). Once loaded, new vintages then refresh automatically in the background.
article(s) still unknown after the offli: article(s) still unknown after the offline detector.
No articles are missing a language — not: No articles are missing a language — nothing to detect.
Lost contact with the job — see the task: Lost contact with the job — see the task manager.
The local model is unavailable (Ollama d: The local model is unavailable (Ollama down or airplane mode).
Resume to continue where it left off.
Backup verified — the set is complete an: Backup verified — the set is complete and intact.
NOT recoverable — the backup is incomple: NOT recoverable — the backup is incomplete
The corpus key is destroyed — the encryp: The corpus key is destroyed — the encrypted store is now permanently unrecoverable.
For defence-in-depth against forensic re: For defence-in-depth against forensic recovery, you can also overwrite the freed disk space. This is optional — the corpus is already cryptographically unrecoverable.
Your store was not encrypted, so a full: Your store was not encrypted, so a full disk-overwrite is recommended before you stop.
This may take several minutes on a large: This may take several minutes on a large disk. Restart the app when you are done.
